attacking_style: Launches decisive solo attacks at 25-30km to go on final classified climbs (Redoute, late-stage summit finishes), or employs sustained tempo riding to drop rivals without explosive accelerations. Rarely attacks earlier unless breakaway hunting after abandoning GC.
team_tactics: Frequently races with minimal domestique support in critical moments, relying on personal power rather than team trains. Quick-Step/Soudal often prioritizes other objectives (breaks, sprints) leaving him isolated on climbs. When supported, team sets sustained tempo rather than explosive launches.
terrain_preference: Dominates time trials of any length (45s-48s margins common) and sustained 10-15km climbs at threshold pace. Excels on descents for time recovery. Vulnerable on extreme gradients (15%+) and short, punchy repeated climbs. Technical wet conditions expose positioning weaknesses.
vulnerability: Cracks under sustained attacks on steep gradients (15%+) when isolated, especially in heat or adverse conditions. Shows mental fragility when racing conditions deteriorate (darkness, crashes). Late-race climbing durability questionable in third week, requiring descent skills to recover from being dropped.
key_patterns: Crashed multiple times in wet/technical conditions across 2022-2023 seasons, suggesting persistent positioning deficiencies.
Delivers negative-split time trial efforts under pressure, dominating intermediate checks then extending advantages.
Hunts bonus seconds aggressively on non-key stages, revealing opportunistic time-gathering even when tactically questionable.
Shows dramatic form swings within same race (Stage 13 collapse to Stage 14 dominance Vuelta 2023).
Operates effectively without teammates on final climbs but visibly struggles when team abandons support early.
Responds to psychological provocations with immediate physical dominance, converting mental edge to acceleration.
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed vulnerability after Lombardia crash and mixed Vuelta form with catastrophic collapses followed by dominant stage wins, suggesting incomplete recovery or metabolic inconsistency despite Giro success earlier in season
